ops.title: capt-hook v9 plugin-canonical migration (Jul 11 2026) silently kills ALL hook dispatch in sessions started before the plugin shipped hooks.json — restart the session, don't debug the pack
ops.body:
On 2026-07-11 ~11:15–11:21 PDT the capt-hook v9 "plugin-canonical" migration removed every `uvx capt-hook run <Event>` entry from repo `.claude/settings.json` files (cc-notes 41c1ff4d, claude-pool 31ddfd1b; upstream captain-hook ea1984f9 moved registration into the plugin's shipped `hooks/hooks.json` and deleted `register-hooks`). The first plugin build actually shipping a hooks.json was installed 2026-07-11 13:26 PDT (f6764eb2).

Because plugin hooks load only at session start while settings-hook removal reaches running sessions, any Claude session started before 2026-07-11 13:26 PDT has ZERO capt-hook dispatch (no PermissionRequest, no nudges, no gates) until restarted. Symptom: teammate/subagent Bash permission prompts pop in the lead UI again — looks like "the fixes pack stopped working," but the pack is healthy.

**Why:** the fixes pack's one hook answers PermissionRequest dialogs; if the event never reaches capt-hook, no decision is returned and the dialog shows. Verified healthy synthetically: piping a teammate-shaped PermissionRequest JSON into `uvx capt-hook run PermissionRequest` from the repo returns `{"behavior": "allow"}`.

**How to apply:** when capt-hook behavior "stops working," first check the session's start time against plugin-cache mtimes (`~/.claude/plugins/cache/captain-hook/captain-hook/`) before debugging pack code — the cure is restarting the stale session. Also: capt-hook logs (`~/.cache/captain-hook/logs/`) are 0-byte since the 8.19.0 "startup diet" (Jul 10 2026), so absence of log lines proves nothing; and the once-guard never applies to PermissionRequest (DECISION_EVENTS, cli.py). Related: [[capt-hook-is-cc-notes-hooks-upstream]], [[capt-hook-5-renamed-command-to-parsedcommand]].
